visualization.description: Grouped bar chart showing average, median, and standard deviation values of satisfaction score, churn score, and CLTV for different customer statuses ('Stayed', 'Churned', 'Joined'). Color coding differentiates customer statuses.

effect.description: Filter data by selected customer status to focus subsequent analyses.

visualization.description: Stacked bar charts comparing demographic distributions like gender, age group, marital status, etc., for each customer status group.
Scatter plot visualizing the relationship between satisfaction score and churn score, distinguished by customer status. Circle size indicates Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV).

effect.description: Select a range to highlight specific customer groups and update drill-down analysis view.

visualization.description: Heatmap showing customer status distributions geographically by city and zip code. Allows exploration through zoom and pan.

effect.description: Zoom into specific areas on the map to explore customer distributions.

visualization.description: Box plots showing distributions of satisfaction and churn scores for each customer status group, highlighting outliers explicitly.
Summarized text-based report highlighting anomalies or extreme cases identified, including detailed reasoning.

outputs.path: The user browses View 1 at the overview level and learns about the overall distribution of customer statuses by key metrics like satisfaction score, churn score, and CLTV in the database. Next, the user selects a specific customer status in View 1 via clicking, which triggers filtering in View 2 and View 3 at the exploration level. In View 2, the user explores demographic attributes like age and gender for the selected customer status group. In View 3, the user further investigates the relationships among satisfaction score, churn score, and CLTV. The findings are further detailed in View 4 at the detail level, showing geographic clusters based on city and zip code. Additionally, the user accesses View 5 for an in-depth understanding of score distributions and potential outliers within each customer group.
